meaning: n.花彩vt.结彩于,使成为花彩形
examples: Each pendent twig and leafy festoon was in a blaze.
All humans festoon their talk tic-style with assurances of sincerity such as "really" and "totally."
The transaction had felt flirtatious to him, and the atmosphere of the downtown, beneath its drooping festoon of useless cables, seemed festive.
On November 13th he called for a nine-point European pact on the future of employment, including crash programmes to expand power grids and to festoon the countryside with broadband networks.
